history.content:
<thought>
I’m facing upward, and the cell directly in front of me is a safe floor cell. Moving forward brings me closer to the open path above, while turning would waste a move.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m still facing upward. The cell directly ahead is now a wall boundary, so I can’t move forward. The only safe move is to turn toward the open corridor to continue navigating around the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible at row 05 column C. I am facing upward, and the cell directly in front of me is a lava cell at row 06 column C, so moving forward would be deadly. I should turn to avoid the lava and look for a safe route around it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at the lower-left open cell inside the corridor, facing up. The cell in front is safe, while the right side has lava, so the best immediate move is to go forward toward the open space that can lead around to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is directly to my right, and the cell in front is blocked by a wall. I should turn right to face the goal and then move toward it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is now two cells ahead and one cell to the right from my position, but there is lava directly to my right, so I should not turn right. The path ahead is clear, so moving forward is the safest step toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is directly ahead, but I can see that the cell in front of me is the goal and the cell to my right is lava. Since moving forward reaches the goal safely, I should advance.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD</action>
